channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: G7 allies are moving ahead with a $50 billion loan for Ukraine backed by frozen Russian funds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ukraine will receive $50 billion in loans, backed by frozen Russian assets, from Group of Seven allies, the White House said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A hurricane's historic destruction hasn't stopped North Carolina election workers and voters
item.description: HOT SPRINGS, N.C. (AP) — About three weeks after Hurricane Helene pummeled western North Carolina, the Hot Springs Community Center was still caked in mud. A paper sign hung on the door warning visitors to use “extreme caution” until the building could be inspected.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee man had enough after SUV broken into 5 times in 3 years
item.description: "I have been in the Home Depot parking sleeping because I'm out of hours and I'm hearing noise. They were out there doing donuts and breaking into cars," said D'Juan Terry.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: Despite E. coli outbreak, McDonald's says customers should feel confident eating at chain
item.description: McDonald’s U.S. President Joe Erlinger said in an interview on the “Today” show that the company has removed the Quarter Pounder from its menu in the 10 states where dozens of people were sickened, as well as in several other states.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump slams 'jerk' Obama as former president campaigns for Harris
item.description: The former president remains especially popular among Democrats, and he has used his appearances to try and appeal directly to Black men and to make the case that Trump is unfit to lead.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: They made 36 million Corn Poppers. Here's how they pick the right ones for the Toy Hall of Fame
item.description: ROCHESTER, N.Y. (AP) — When curators at the National Toy Hall of Fame learned last fall that the Fisher-Price Corn Popper had been voted in as part of the class of 2023, they knew they had some serious work to do.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Exhibit traces cowboy roots from Mexico to Hawaii
item.description: "It's the weaving of multiple cultures," said Bill Lawrence, president and CEO of the San Diego History Center. "The vaqueros are the basis for the American cowboy, so Texas is absolutely part of this story.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Missing teen hiker in California reunited with family after spending night in sub-freezing temperature
item.description: A 16-year-old hiker who went missing during a hike with her dog in Northern California on Sunday has been found safe, search and rescuers said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump hurls string of insults at Harris including racist trope against Black people
item.description: Trump signaled he will lean harder into disparaging Harris during the remaining two weeks before Election Day, despite the urging of allies who have repeatedly suggested he should steer clear of personal attacks including references to her race and gender.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Illinois jail continues allowing detainees to vote in presidential elections, other local races
item.description: The Cook County Jail in Chicago is teaching civics classes to those detained. They then have a chance to study their options and vote from the jail's chapel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Growing number of men vs. Diddy lawsuits 'part of industry': Ray J
item.description: "It's a power trip, and it hits hard. I know a few celebs who have been in that crazy situation," Ray J said on "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Yelp turns off comments on the McDonald's that Trump worked at after fake reviews
item.description: Yelp recently paused reviews on a McDonald's in Pennsylvania after former President Trump served pre-screened customers at the location Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man killed in harrowing Houston helicopter crash identified, remembered as humble family man
item.description: A man who worked two doors down from Cesar Lerma for more than a decade talks with KPRC 2's Deven Clark.
item.dc:creator: Deven Clarke, Adrian Crooks, Karen Araiza, KPRC 2

item.title: MS-13 gang member convicted for ‘gruesome murders' in Northern Virginia, Massachusetts
item.description: Elmer De Jesus Alas Candray faces life behind bars after being convicted of killing six people between 2018 and 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Capitol Christmas Tree is coming to Utah as part of its 4,000-mile trek -- Here's how it's staying alive
item.description: Here's what you should know about this year's Capitol Christmas Tree — and how the tree will be kept alive during its travels.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Rep. Brandon Williams and challenger John Mannion square off in NY-22 debate
item.description: Debate between candidates in the 22nd Congressional District. Featuring incumbent Brandon Williams and challenger NY State Senator John Mannion.
item.dc:creator: Dave Longley, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina county surrounding Asheville overcounted Helene deaths by as many as 30, sheriff says
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The North Carolina county that is home to Asheville overcounted deaths caused by Helene by as many as 30, according to a statement Tuesday from its sheriff’s office and data from the state, significantly reducing the death toll from the historic storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McDonald's Quarter Pounders linked to E. coli outbreak across multiple states, CDC says
item.description: The McDonald's Quarter Pounder has been linked to an E. coli outbreak across 10 states in which one person has died, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Severe E. coli outbreak linked to McDonald's quarter pounders
item.description: The CDC has issued a food safety alert for an outbreak of E. coli infections linked to McDonald's quarter pounders, with 49 people from ten states affected and one death reported.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Grieving families of seniors killed when a dock collapsed say the tragedy was preventable
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Regina Brinson heard a crack before the metal walkway gave way beneath her feet, plunging her into the water beneath the state-operated ferry dock on Georgia's Sapelo Island.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Richard N. Winfield, First Amendment lawyer and former AP counsel, dies at 91
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Richard N. Winfield, a leading U.S. First Amendment lawyer who represented The Associated Press for three decades and championed freedom of expression for journalists around the world, died on Tuesday. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US charges Iran Revolutionary Guard official in alleged plot to kill a human rights activist in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal prosecutors on Tuesday charged a senior Iranian military official and three others with links to that country's government with plotting to kill an Iranian American author on U.S. soil — a development lauded by the alleged target. “This is a beautiful day for me.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A Georgia teen accused in the Apalachee High School shooting has pleaded not guilty
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A 14-year-old Georgia boy indicted on murder charges in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School pleaded not guilty on Tuesday. Colt Gray's lawyer filed papers entering the plea after Gray was indicted on Thursday. They waived an arraignment hearing that had been scheduled for Nov.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders Giuliani to hand over assets to Georgia election workers
item.description: Rudy Giuliani must turn over much of his property — his apartment in New York, the city that made him mayor, and other prized possessions — to two Georgia election workers who have sought for months to collect their massive defamation judgment, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, Fox 5

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in Walmart oven
item.description: A Walmart store in eastern Canada was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven. Halifax Nova Scotia Regional Police said in a release that they were called at 9:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Jocelina Joiner, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge hears arguments to block Louisiana's Ten Commandments displays in schools
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge heard arguments at a hearing Monday on whether he should temporarily block a new Louisiana law that requires the Ten Commandments to be displayed in every public school classroom by Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopter crashes into radio tower in Houston, killing 4
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Four people aboard a helicopter were killed when it crashed into a radio tower in Houston and burst into flames, officials said. The helicopter went down just before 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pinellas County man recovering after near-death experience with flesh-eating bacteria
item.description: A Pinellas County man is sharing his near death experience after contracting Vibrio Vulnificus, a flesh-eating bacteria. This comes as the Florida Department of Health is warning people to avoid floodwaters, as cases of the infection continue to rise after back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald's says it doesn't endorse political candidates after Trump visit
item.description: McDonald's is emphasizing its political neutrality after former President Donald Trump spent some time behind the counter at one of its Pennsylvania locations.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, Fox 5

item.title: Paul Di'Anno, former Iron Maiden lead singer, dies at 66
item.description: Former Iron Maiden singer Paul Andrews, better known by his stage name Paul Di'Anno, has died at the age of 66.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KRON4

item.title: How does abortion translate? Ballot measures are a challenge for interpreters
item.description: Reproductive rights measures are on the ballots in 10 states after heated debates over how to describe their impact on abortion — and that's just in English. In 388 places across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL WARREN, GEOFF MULVIHILL and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harris raises more than $220 million in September, outraising Trump 3-to-1
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris’s campaign raised nearly $222 million in September, raising more than three times as much as former President Donald Trump’s campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Learning about God from dogs, Orthodox monks breed and train canines in upstate New York monastery
item.description: CAMBRIDGE, New York (AP) — After he and his fellow monks sang morning prayers in their church nestled in a forest, Brother Luke walked back to his residence to be greeted by a different kind of choir.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4
